name_policy: Personal names remain in original English.
spoiler_policy: Only information available through each current paragraph is used.

blocks.original_text: They had taken shelter beneath a copse of aspens just off the high road. Tyrion was gathering deadwood while their horses took water from a mountain stream. He stooped to pick up a splintered branch and examined it critically. “Will this do? I am not practiced at starting fires. Morrec did that for me.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion与Bronn在high road旁aspen林歇脚；他捡deadwood并承认不熟悉生火。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 曾有Morrec生火的Tyrion如今亲自拾柴，身份落差被写成弯腰、back ache与不熟练的具体劳动。
blocks.background_zh: **high road：** Bloody Gate至riverlands的山路，狭险且受mountain clans袭扰；Lysa让两人带物资离开却不提供全程保护。

blocks.original_text: “A fire?” Bronn said, spitting. “Are you so hungry to die, dwarf? Or have you taken leave of your senses? A fire will bring the clansmen down on us from miles around. I mean to survive this journey, Lannister.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn认为fire会从远处招来clansmen，斥Tyrion在求死。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Bronn把fire只视为visibility risk；Tyrion随后把它当诱饵、士气和谈判舞台，同一物件承担相反策略。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “And how do you hope to do that?” Tyrion asked. He tucked the branch under his arm and poked around through the sparse undergrowth, looking for more. His back ached from the effort of bending; they had been riding since daybreak, when a stone-faced Ser Lyn Corbray had ushered them through the Bloody Gate and commanded them never to return.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion继续弯腰拾柴，并反问Bronn如何活下去；两人清晨已被Corbray逐出Bloody Gate。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion拒绝Bronn夜行隐蔽方案，以fire、food和可预见接触重设high road生存策略；两人坦白关系只基于利益。
blocks.background_zh: **放逐：** Ser Lyn奉Lysa命把Tyrion、Bronn送出Bloody Gate并禁止返回Vale核心区域。

blocks.original_text: “We have no chance of fighting our way back,” Bronn said, “but two can cover more ground than ten, and attract less notice. The fewer days we spend in these mountains, the more like we are to reach the riverlands. Ride hard and fast, I say. Travel by night and hole up by day, avoid the road where we can, make no noise and light no fires.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn主张两人轻装夜行、白天躲藏、避路禁火，以速度和隐蔽穿越。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Bronn方案符合小队隐蔽常识，却低估夜间山路、马力与clan观察网；合理不等于适合全部约束。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion Lannister sighed. “A splendid plan, Bronn. Try it, as you like . . . and forgive me if I do not linger to bury you.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion讽刺计划看似漂亮，却预言Bronn会先死。
blocks.literary_note_zh: forgive me if I do not linger to bury you以礼貌句式包装死亡预测，是Tyrion惯用的防御性幽默。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “You think to outlive me, dwarf?” The sellsword grinned. He had a dark gap in his smile where the edge of Ser Vardis Egen’s shield had cracked a tooth in half.
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn露出Vardis盾击留下的断牙，反问Tyrion是否真能活更久。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion拒绝Bronn夜行隐蔽方案，以fire、food和可预见接触重设high road生存策略；两人坦白关系只基于利益。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion shrugged. “Riding hard and fast by night is a sure way to tumble down a mountain and crack your skull. I prefer to make my crossing slow and easy. I know you love the taste of horse, Bronn, but if our mounts die under us this time, we’ll be trying to saddle shadowcats . . . and if truth be told, I think the clans will find us no matter what we do. Their eyes are all around us.” He swept a gloved hand over the high, wind-carved crags that surrounded them.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion指出夜骑山路易坠崖、马匹会累死，且clans无论如何都在观察，选择慢行。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion不是证明慢行安全，而是指出隐蔽方案也有致命代价，并接受clans必然找到他们的前提。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: Bronn grimaced. “Then we’re dead men, Lannister.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn由此判断两人已经是dead men。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion拒绝Bronn夜行隐蔽方案，以fire、food和可预见接触重设high road生存策略；两人坦白关系只基于利益。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “If so, I prefer to die comfortable,” Tyrion replied. “We need a fire. The nights are cold up here, and hot food will warm our bellies and lift our spirits. Do you suppose there’s any game to be had? Lady Lysa has kindly provided us with a veritable feast of salt beef, hard cheese, and stale bread, but I would hate to break a tooth so far from the nearest maester.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion宁愿舒适地死，认为fire、热食与士气比隐蔽更重要，并嘲笑Lysa给的硬粮。
blocks.literary_note_zh: die comfortable听似享乐主义，实则维持warmth、food与morale；在无力避敌时，他把目标从逃避接触改为准备接触。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “I can find meat.” Beneath a fall of black hair, Bronn’s dark eyes regarded Tyrion suspiciously. “I should leave you here with your fool’s fire. If I took your horse, I’d have twice the chance to make it through. What would you do then, dwarf?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn说自己能hunt，又试探若带走Tyrion的horse独逃会怎样。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Bronn公开试探abandonment既是威胁也是合同谈判：他要求Tyrion证明合作收益高于独逃。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “Die, most like.” Tyrion stooped to get another stick.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion平静回答自己大概会死。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion拒绝Bronn夜行隐蔽方案，以fire、food和可预见接触重设high road生存策略；两人坦白关系只基于利益。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “You don’t think I’d do it?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn问他是否不相信自己真会这样做。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion拒绝Bronn夜行隐蔽方案，以fire、food和可预见接触重设high road生存策略；两人坦白关系只基于利益。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “You’d do it in an instant, if it meant your life. You were quick enough to silence your friend Chiggen when he caught that arrow in his belly.” Bronn had yanked back the man’s head by the hair and driven the point of his dirk in under the ear, and afterward told Catelyn Stark that the other sellsword had died of his wound.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion相信Bronn会为生存立刻背弃，并以他结束重伤Chiggen生命为证。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion准确读出Bronn的生存伦理；提Chiggen也让‘会背叛’从抽象评价落到已发生行为。
blocks.background_zh: **Chiggen：** high road伏击中腹部中箭的sellsword；Bronn为停止呻吟和吸引攻击而结束其生命，并对Catelyn隐瞒。

blocks.original_text: “He was good as dead,” Bronn said, “and his moaning was bringing them down on us. Chiggen would have done the same for me . . . and he was no friend, only a man I rode with. Make no mistake, dwarf. I fought for you, but I do not love you.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn说Chiggen必死且呻吟会引敌，双方只是同行者，并明确自己替Tyrion战斗却不爱他。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Bronn把mercy killing、noise discipline与friendship否认压在一起；他的原则冷酷却可预测。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “It was your blade I needed,” Tyrion said, “not your love.” He dumped his armful of wood on the ground.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion说自己要的是Bronn的blade，不是love。
blocks.literary_note_zh: blade not love确立双方交易型关系，也区别于封建忠诚与私人友爱。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: Bronn grinned. “You’re bold as any sellsword, I’ll give you that. How did you know I’d take your part?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn称赞Tyrion像sellsword一样大胆，追问他如何知道自己会出战。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion解释自己如何押中Bronn会做champion，并用持续报酬、匹配报价与不要求跪拜建立雇佣合同。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “Know?” Tyrion squatted awkwardly on his stunted legs to build the fire. “I tossed the dice. Back at the inn, you and Chiggen helped take me captive. Why? The others saw it as their duty, for the honor of the lords they served, but not you two. You had no lord, no duty, and precious little honor, so why trouble to involve yourselves?” He took out his knife and whittled some thin strips of bark off one of the sticks he’d gathered, to serve as kindling. “Well, why do sellswords do anything? For gold. You were thinking Lady Catelyn would reward you for your help, perhaps even take you into her service. Here, that should do, I hope. Do you have a flint?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion说只是tossed dice；他从inn上Bronn帮助Catelyn判断其动机不是duty或honor，而是gold。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion重构inn行为的incentive：同一帮捕动作在knights是duty，在sellswords是预期reward。
blocks.background_zh: **inn选择：** Bronn、Chiggen本无lord oath，却在Catelyn号召下协助拘捕Tyrion；Tyrion事后以expected reward解释。

blocks.original_text: Bronn slid two fingers into the pouch at his belt and tossed down a flint. Tyrion caught it in the air.
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn把flint扔给Tyrion。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion解释自己如何押中Bronn会做champion，并用持续报酬、匹配报价与不要求跪拜建立雇佣合同。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “My thanks,” he said. “The thing is, you did not know the Starks. Lord Eddard is a proud, honorable, and honest man, and his lady wife is worse. Oh, no doubt she would have found a coin or two for you when this was all over, and pressed it in your hand with a polite word and a look of distaste, but that’s the most you could have hoped for. The Starks look for courage and loyalty and honor in the men they choose to serve them, and if truth be told, you and Chiggen were lowborn scum.” Tyrion struck the flint against his dagger, trying for a spark. Nothing.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion解释Starks只会小额酬谢且厌恶无honor的sellswords，自己才是Bronn更有利的买主；第一次打火失败。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Starks评价既含洞察也带Tyrion阶级偏见；他用lowborn scum刺激Bronn，同时证明Lannister市场更匹配。
blocks.background_zh: **Stark service：** Tyrion认为Starks偏好courage、loyalty、honor，不会长期收纳Bronn型sellsword；这是他的判断，不是正式政策。

blocks.original_text: Bronn snorted. “You have a bold tongue, little man. One day someone is like to cut it out and make you eat it.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn警告Tyrion的bold tongue迟早被割。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion解释自己如何押中Bronn会做champion，并用持续报酬、匹配报价与不要求跪拜建立雇佣合同。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “Everyone tells me that.” Tyrion glanced up at the sellsword. “Did I offend you? My pardons . . . but you are scum, Bronn, make no mistake. Duty, honor, friendship, what’s that to you? No, don’t trouble yourself, we both know the answer. Still, you’re not stupid. Once we reached the Vale, Lady Stark had no more need of you . . . but I did, and the one thing the Lannisters have never lacked for is gold. When the moment came to toss the dice, I was counting on your being smart enough to know where your best interest lay. Happily for me, you did.” He slammed stone and steel together again, fruitlessly.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion继续直称Bronn是scum，却说明他聪明地发现Catelyn不再需要自己、Tyrion仍需要且Lannisters有gold。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 第二次打火仍失败与言辞成功并置：他能点燃Bronn的利益，却点不燃bark，需要对方补足实际技能。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “Here,” said Bronn, squatting, “I’ll do it.” He took the knife and flint from Tyrion’s hands and struck sparks on his first try. A curl of bark began to smolder.
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn接手knife与flint，一次便点燃bark。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Bronn一击出spark把他们的互补关系具象化：Tyrion提供策略与资源，Bronn提供技术与暴力。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “Well done,” Tyrion said. “Scum you may be, but you’re undeniably useful, and with a sword in your hand you’re almost as good as my brother Jaime. What do you want, Bronn? Gold? Land? Women? Keep me alive, and you’ll have it.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion称他粗鄙但有用、剑术近Jaime，承诺只要保自己活着便给gold、land或women。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion解释自己如何押中Bronn会做champion，并用持续报酬、匹配报价与不要求跪拜建立雇佣合同。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: Bronn blew gently on the fire, and the flames leapt up higher. “And if you die?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn一边吹旺火一边问若Tyrion死了如何。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion解释自己如何押中Bronn会做champion，并用持续报酬、匹配报价与不要求跪拜建立雇佣合同。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “Why then, I’ll have one mourner whose grief is sincere,” Tyrion said, grinning. “The gold ends when I do.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion笑说那样Bronn会成为真心哀悼者，因为报酬也随之消失。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 报酬绑定Tyrion存活，形成最简单的incentive alignment；Bronn的真心悲伤不是爱，而是未来收益消失。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: The fire was blazing up nicely. Bronn stood, tucked the flint back into his pouch, and tossed Tyrion his dagger. “Fair enough,” he said. “My sword’s yours, then . . . but don’t go looking for me to bend the knee and m’lord you every time you take a shit. I’m no man’s toady.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn接受交易，声明sword归Tyrion使用，但不跪、不逢迎，也不做toady。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Bronn出售service但拒绝identity surrender；不bend knee划出雇佣合同与vassalage边界。
blocks.background_zh: **雇佣关系：** Bronn提供sword service换取未来报酬，不宣誓fealty；双方都公开承认可因利益变化而终止。

blocks.original_text: “Nor any man’s friend,” Tyrion said. “I’ve no doubt you’d betray me as quick as you did Lady Stark, if you saw a profit in it. If the day ever comes when you’re tempted to sell me out, remember this, Bronn—I’ll match their price, whatever it is. I like living. And now, do you think you could do something about finding us some supper?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion承认Bronn会为更高利润背叛，并预先承诺匹配任何报价，随后要他找supper。
blocks.literary_note_zh: match their price把未来背叛风险转成竞价问题，但并不能防止非金钱动机或Tyrion无力支付。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “Take care of the horses,” Bronn said, unsheathing the long dirk he wore at his hip. He strode into the trees.
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn让Tyrion照顾horses，持dirk进林hunt。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion解释自己如何押中Bronn会做champion，并用持续报酬、匹配报价与不要求跪拜建立雇佣合同。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: An hour later the horses had been rubbed down and fed, the fire was crackling away merrily, and a haunch of a young goat was turning above the flames, spitting and hissing. “All we lack now is some good wine to wash down our kid,” Tyrion said.
blocks.explanation_zh: 一小时后马已照料、火已升起，young goat在烤；Tyrion只遗憾没有wine。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 营火、goat与磨剑形成诱饵；Tyrion等待clansmen时谈Mord债务、dagger问题与一首Myrish旧歌。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “That, a woman, and another dozen swords,” Bronn said. He sat cross-legged beside the fire, honing the edge of his longsword with an oilstone. There was something strangely reassuring about the rasping sound it made when he drew it down the steel. “It will be full dark soon,” the sellsword pointed out. “I’ll take first watch . . . for all the good it will do us. It might be kinder to let them kill us in our sleep.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn还想要woman与十二把swords；磨剑声给Tyrion奇异安心，他又悲观地安排watch。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 磨剑声让Tyrion安心，说明信任并非相信Bronn善良，而是相信他会维护仍有价值的investment。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “Oh, I imagine they’ll be here long before it comes to sleep.” The smell of the roasting meat made Tyrion’s mouth water.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion认为clansmen睡前就会出现。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 营火、goat与磨剑形成诱饵；Tyrion等待clansmen时谈Mord债务、dagger问题与一首Myrish旧歌。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: Bronn watched him across the fire. “You have a plan,” he said flatly, with a scrape of steel on stone.
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn看出他另有plan。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 营火、goat与磨剑形成诱饵；Tyrion等待clansmen时谈Mord债务、dagger问题与一首Myrish旧歌。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “A hope, call it,” Tyrion said. “Another toss of the dice.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion称其只是又一次hope与toss of dice。
blocks.literary_note_zh: dice motif从Eyrie courtroom延续到mountain：Tyrion能改善赔率，不能消除随机与对手选择。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “With our lives as the stake?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn确认赌注是两人性命。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 营火、goat与磨剑形成诱饵；Tyrion等待clansmen时谈Mord债务、dagger问题与一首Myrish旧歌。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion shrugged. “What choice do we have?” He leaned over the fire and sawed a thin slice of meat from the kid. “Ahhhh,” he sighed happily as he chewed. Grease ran down his chin. “A bit tougher than I’d like, and in want of spicing, but I’ll not complain too loudly. If I were back at the Eyrie, I’d be dancing on a precipice in hopes of a boiled bean.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion说别无选择，享用烤肉并拿sky cell的boiled bean反衬当前满足。
blocks.literary_note_zh: boiled bean回扣sky cell饥饿；当前粗硬goat因比较基准变化成为幸福。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “And yet you gave the turnkey a purse of gold,” Bronn said.
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn指出Tyrion仍给了Mord一袋gold。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 营火、goat与磨剑形成诱饵；Tyrion等待clansmen时谈Mord债务、dagger问题与一首Myrish旧歌。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “A Lannister always pays his debts.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion说Lannister always pays his debts。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 家族格言式句子把兑现Mord reward变成reputation mechanism：未来承诺可信，才能继续买到危险中的帮助。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: Even Mord had scarcely believed it when Tyrion tossed him the leather purse. The gaoler’s eyes had gone big as boiled eggs as he yanked open the drawstring and beheld the glint of gold. “I kept the silver,” Tyrion had told him with a crooked smile, “but you were promised the gold, and there it is.” It was more than a man like Mord could hope to earn in a lifetime of abusing prisoners. “And remember what I said, this is only a taste. If you ever grow tired of Lady Arryn’s service, present yourself at Casterly Rock, and I’ll pay you the rest of what I owe you.” With golden dragons spilling out of both hands, Mord had fallen to his knees and promised that he would do just that.
blocks.explanation_zh: 回忆中Tyrion兑现书面承诺，把gold给惊讶的Mord，还邀请他日后到Casterly Rock取余款。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 付清gold也把曾虐待自己的Mord转成潜在future asset；Tyrion的报复常延后，交易可先于情绪。
blocks.background_zh: **Mord debt：** Tyrion曾书面许诺gold换取向Lysa传递confession；获释后按约付清，并承诺未来余款。

blocks.original_text: Bronn yanked out his dirk and pulled the meat from the fire. He began to carve thick chunks of charred meat off the bone as Tyrion hollowed out two heels of stale bread to serve as trenchers. “If we do reach the river, what will you do then?” the sellsword asked as he cut.
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn切肉，Tyrion用stale bread做trenchers；Bronn问抵达river后做什么。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 营火、goat与磨剑形成诱饵；Tyrion等待clansmen时谈Mord债务、dagger问题与一首Myrish旧歌。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “Oh, a whore and a featherbed and a flagon of wine, for a start.” Tyrion held out his trencher, and Bronn filled it with meat. “And then to Casterly Rock or King’s Landing, I think. I have some questions that want answering, concerning a certain dagger.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion先想享乐，再去Casterly Rock或King’s Landing调查某把dagger。
blocks.literary_note_zh: dagger使本章生存线重新连接Bran刺杀谜团；Tyrion仍只有questions，没有答案。
blocks.background_zh: **dagger mystery：** Catelyn认为Bran袭击所用dagger属于Tyrion；Tyrion否认并准备追查真正来源。

blocks.original_text: The sellsword chewed and swallowed. “So you were telling it true? It was not your knife?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn确认Tyrion此前说实话，knife确实不是他的。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 营火、goat与磨剑形成诱饵；Tyrion等待clansmen时谈Mord债务、dagger问题与一首Myrish旧歌。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion smiled thinly. “Do I look a liar to you?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion以反问玩笑回应自己是否像liar。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Do I look a liar既是玩笑也是回避：Bronn需要根据共同经历而非Tyrion自我声明判断。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: By the time their bellies were full, the stars had come out and a halfmoon was rising over the mountains. Tyrion spread his shadowskin cloak on the ground and stretched out with his saddle for a pillow. “Our friends are taking their sweet time.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 夜深后Tyrion以shadowskin与saddle躺下，抱怨clansmen迟迟不来。
blocks.literary_note_zh: friends是反讽称呼；Tyrion故意把clansmen预设为可谈对象，先在语言中改变关系框架。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “If I were them, I’d fear a trap,” Bronn said. “Why else would we be so open, if not to lure them in?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn说对方可能怀疑如此公开的营地是trap。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 公开营火造成signal ambiguity：既可能是愚蠢，也可能是trap；Tyrion用对方谨慎为自己争取准备时间。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion chuckled. “Then we ought to sing and send them fleeing in terror.” He began to whistle a tune.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion开玩笑应唱歌把他们吓走，开始whistle。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 营火、goat与磨剑形成诱饵；Tyrion等待clansmen时谈Mord债务、dagger问题与一首Myrish旧歌。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “You’re mad, dwarf,” Bronn said as he cleaned the grease out from under his nails with his dirk.
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn说Tyrion发疯了，同时用dirk清指甲。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 营火、goat与磨剑形成诱饵；Tyrion等待clansmen时谈Mord债务、dagger问题与一首Myrish旧歌。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “Where’s your love of music, Bronn?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion问他为何不爱music。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 营火、goat与磨剑形成诱饵；Tyrion等待clansmen时谈Mord债务、dagger问题与一首Myrish旧歌。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “If it was music you wanted, you should have gotten the singer to champion you.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn说想听音乐就该让singer当champion。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 营火、goat与磨剑形成诱饵；Tyrion等待clansmen时谈Mord债务、dagger问题与一首Myrish旧歌。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion grinned. “That would have been amusing. I can just see him fending off Ser Vardis with his woodharp.” He resumed his whistling. “Do you know this song?” he asked.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion想象Marillion用woodharp抵挡Vardis，继续吹曲并问Bronn是否认识。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 用woodharp挡剑的荒诞图像缓解等待恐惧，也把Marillion传播功能与Bronn实战功能再次区分。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “You hear it here and there, in inns and whorehouses.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn说inns与whorehouses偶尔会听到。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 营火、goat与磨剑形成诱饵；Tyrion等待clansmen时谈Mord债务、dagger问题与一首Myrish旧歌。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “Myrish. ‘The Seasons of My Love.’ Sweet and sad, if you understand the words. The first girl I ever bedded used to sing it, and I’ve never been able to put it out of my head.” Tyrion gazed up at the sky. It was a clear cold night and the stars shone down upon the mountains as bright and merciless as truth. “I met her on a night like this,” he heard himself saying. “Jaime and I were riding back from Lannisport when we heard a scream, and she came running out into the road with two men dogging her heels, shouting threats. My brother unsheathed his sword and went after them, while I dismounted to protect the girl. She was scarcely a year older than I was, dark-haired, slender, with a face that would break your heart. It certainly broke mine. Lowborn, half-starved, unwashed . . . yet lovely. They’d torn the rags she was wearing half off her back, so I wrapped her in my cloak while Jaime chased the men into the woods. By the time he came trotting back, I’d gotten a name out of her, and a story. She was a crofter’s child, orphaned when her father died of fever, on her way to . . . well, nowhere, really.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion认出Myrish歌The Seasons of My Love，并开始讲十三岁时与Jaime救下一个约大一岁的孤女。
blocks.literary_note_zh: stars bright and merciless as truth触发回忆；‘truth’既指Bronn刚问dagger，也预告Tyrion生命中被权威告知的另一种真相。
blocks.background_zh: **年龄事实：** Tyrion自称当时十三岁，女孩scarcely a year older，约十四岁；因此相关露骨内容不复录。

blocks.original_text: “Jaime was all in a lather to hunt down the men. It was not often outlaws dared prey on travelers so near to Casterly Rock, and he took it as an insult. The girl was too frightened to send off by herself, though, so I offered to take her to the closest inn and feed her while my brother rode back to the Rock for help.
blocks.explanation_zh: Jaime想追outlaws；Tyrion留下照顾受惊女孩，带她去inn进食。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 歌曲触发Tyrion十三岁秘密婚姻及其被Tywin暴力摧毁的创伤回忆；睡梦中他反转sky-cell权力，攻击father。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: [原文涉及十三岁的Tyrion与约十四岁女孩之间的露骨性描写，此处不逐字复录。请参见源 PDF p.420 对应段落。]
blocks.explanation_zh: 本段写十三岁的Tyrion与约十四岁女孩发生露骨性关系及其情感反应；不复录细节。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段涉及未成年人露骨性内容，不逐字复录。分析重点是：Tyrion把初次亲密经验与被爱、勇气和song永久绑定。
blocks.background_zh: **安全说明：** 本段涉及两名未成年人的露骨性描写；保留稳定编号、页码、非露骨段意和叙事作用。

blocks.original_text: “You?” Bronn’s voice was amused.
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn对Tyrion会陷入爱情感到好笑。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 歌曲触发Tyrion十三岁秘密婚姻及其被Tywin暴力摧毁的创伤回忆；睡梦中他反转sky-cell权力，攻击father。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “Absurd, isn’t it?” Tyrion began to whistle the song again. “I married her,” he finally admitted.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion自嘲荒谬，随后承认自己娶了她。
blocks.literary_note_zh: I married her延迟到自嘲和whistling之后才说，短句揭开关系对十三岁Tyrion的认真程度。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “A Lannister of Casterly Rock wed to a crofter’s daughter,” Bronn said. “How did you manage that?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn惊讶Casterly Rock的Lannister竟娶crofter’s daughter，追问如何办到。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 歌曲触发Tyrion十三岁秘密婚姻及其被Tywin暴力摧毁的创伤回忆；睡梦中他反转sky-cell权力，攻击father。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “Oh, you’d be astonished at what a boy can make of a few lies, fifty pieces of silver, and a drunken septon. I dared not bring my bride home to Casterly Rock, so I set her up in a cottage of her own, and for a fortnight we played at being man and wife. And then the septon sobered and confessed all to my lord father.” Tyrion was surprised at how desolate it made him feel to say it, even after all these years. Perhaps he was just tired. “That was the end of my marriage.” He sat up and stared at the dying fire, blinking at the light.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion说靠谎言、silver和醉septon秘密成婚并同住fortnight；septon向Tywin告发后婚姻终结，回忆仍令他荒凉。
blocks.literary_note_zh: fortnight的短暂家庭游戏与多年desolate形成时间反差；dying fire把爱情回忆转入毁灭段落。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “He sent the girl away?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn问Tywin是否只是把女孩送走。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 歌曲触发Tyrion十三岁秘密婚姻及其被Tywin暴力摧毁的创伤回忆；睡梦中他反转sky-cell权力，攻击father。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: [原文涉及约十四岁女孩被安排进入商业性关系及Jaime向Tyrion提供的解释，此处不复录具体性内容。请参见源 PDF p.420 对应段落。]
blocks.explanation_zh: 本段涉及约十四岁女孩被安排进入商业性关系及Jaime向Tyrion提供的解释；不复录具体性内容。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段含未成年商业性剥削信息，不复录细节。Tyrion当时被Jaime提供的解释重新定义整段相遇，信任损伤来自‘一切被安排’。
blocks.background_zh: **安全说明：** 本段涉及未成年商业性剥削叙事；不复录具体性内容，也不将付费或身份标签误当consent。

blocks.original_text: [原文涉及Tywin命guards对约十四岁女孩实施集体性暴力，并强迫十三岁的Tyrion观看和参与；此处不逐字复录。请参见源 PDF pp.420–421 对应段落。]
blocks.explanation_zh: 本段描写Tywin命guards对约十四岁女孩实施集体性暴力，并强迫十三岁的Tyrion观看和参与；不复录细节。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 本段含针对未成年人的集体性暴力与强迫参与，不复录细节。行为责任在Tywin及执行者；支付、命令或对女孩身份的指称都不能构成consent。
blocks.background_zh: **安全说明：** 本段涉及对约十四岁女孩的集体性暴力及对十三岁Tyrion的强迫参与；保留事件性质与责任分析，不复录过程。

blocks.original_text: After a time he heard the noise again, the rasp of steel on stone as Bronn sharpened his sword. “Thirteen or thirty or three, I would have killed the man who did that to me.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 长久沉默后Bronn说无论十三、三十还是三岁，自己都会杀死这样对待他的人。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Bronn第一次不用笑或交易回应，而以杀意承认伤害严重性；年龄并不减轻施害者责任。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion swung around to face him. “You may get that chance one day. Remember what I told you. A Lannister always pays his debts.” He yawned. “I think I will try and sleep. Wake me if we’re about to die.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion说Bronn或许终有机会，并用pay debts暗示对Tywin的怨恨，然后准备睡觉。
blocks.literary_note_zh: pay debts从守信口号转为复仇暗语，同一句家族价值同时维系contracts与延续创伤。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: He rolled himself up in the shadowskin and shut his eyes. The ground was stony and cold, but after a time Tyrion Lannister did sleep. He dreamt of the sky cell. This time he was the gaoler, not the prisoner, big, with a strap in his hand, and he was hitting his father, driving him back, toward the abyss . . .
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion梦见自己成为sky-cell gaoler，挥strap把father逼向abyss。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 梦把sky cell角色反转：Tyrion不再是被Mord逼向蓝色，而是拥有strap、身体变大并把Tywin逼向abyss。
blocks.background_zh: **dream continuity：** sky cell曾由Mord持strap把Tyrion逼近悬崖；梦把Mord位置、身体力量和target全部反转。

blocks.original_text: “Tyrion.” Bronn’s warning was low and urgent.
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn低声急促警告Tyrion。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Stone Crows包围营地；Tyrion依次用hospitality、clan language、物资、幽默、羞耻与steel需求把处刑转为联盟谈判。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion was awake in the blink of an eye. The fire had burned down to embers, and the shadows were creeping in all around them. Bronn had raised himself to one knee, his sword in one hand and his dirk in the other. Tyrion held up a hand: stay still, it said. “Come share our fire, the night is cold,” he called out to the creeping shadows. “I fear we’ve no wine to offer you, but you’re welcome to some of our goat.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion瞬间醒来，见Bronn持双刃戒备；他示意不动，主动邀请暗影中的人来分享fire与goat。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 醒来即邀请敌人共享fire说明营火本来就是接触策略；他先给food与hospitality框架，不等对方完成attack。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: All movement stopped. Tyrion saw the glint of moonlight on metal. “Our mountain,” a voice called out from the trees, deep and hard and unfriendly. “Our goat.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 暗处声音宣称mountain和goat都属于他们。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Our mountain, our goat否定lowland property rules；Tyrion要谈判必须先承认对方的territorial premise。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “Your goat,” Tyrion agreed. “Who are you?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion顺势承认goat归属并问对方身份。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Stone Crows包围营地；Tyrion依次用hospitality、clan language、物资、幽默、羞耻与steel需求把处刑转为联盟谈判。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “When you meet your gods,” a different voice replied, “say it was Gunthor son of Gurn of the Stone Crows who sent you to them.” A branch cracked underfoot as he stepped into the light; a thin man in a horned helmet, armed with a long knife.
blocks.explanation_zh: Gunthor son of Gurn持long knife现身，叫Tyrion死后告诉gods是谁送他去。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Gunthor以完整父系name和Stone Crows身份报出，证明clans并非无名bandits，而有自己的荣誉与组织语言。
blocks.background_zh: **Stone Crows：** Mountains of the Moon clan之一；使用父名、自有chief与集体领地观念，不服从Vale lords。

blocks.original_text: “And Shagga son of Dolf.” That was the first voice, deep and deadly. A boulder shifted to their left, and stood, and became a man. Massive and slow and strong he seemed, dressed all in skins, with a club in his right hand and an axe in his left. He smashed them together as he lumbered closer.
blocks.explanation_zh: 巨大的Shagga son of Dolf持club与axe现身，以武器相击逼近。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Stone Crows包围营地；Tyrion依次用hospitality、clan language、物资、幽默、羞耻与steel需求把处刑转为联盟谈判。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: Other voices called other names, Conn and Torrek and Jaggot and more that Tyrion forgot the instant he heard them; ten at least. A few had swords and knives; others brandished pitchforks and scythes and wooden spears. He waited until they were done shouting out their names before he gave them answer. “I am Tyrion son of Tywin, of the Clan Lannister, the Lions of the Rock. We will gladly pay you for the goat we ate.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 至少十名clansmen依次报name；Tyrion等他们结束才以Clan Lannister格式自报并愿赔goat。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion模仿Clan naming把House Lannister翻译成对方可识别的kin group，同时耐心让每人完成自我展示。
blocks.background_zh: **Clan Lannister：** 正式称呼是House Lannister；Tyrion故意改用Clan以匹配Stone Crows的社会语言。

blocks.original_text: “What do you have to give us, Tyrion son of Tywin?” asked the one who named himself Gunthor, who seemed to be their chief.
blocks.explanation_zh: 首领Gunthor问Tyrion能给什么。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Stone Crows包围营地；Tyrion依次用hospitality、clan language、物资、幽默、羞耻与steel需求把处刑转为联盟谈判。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “There is silver in my purse,” Tyrion told them. “This hauberk I wear is large for me, but it should fit Conn nicely, and the battle-axe I carry would suit Shagga’s mighty hand far better than that wood-axe he holds.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion提出silver，并精确把自己的hauberk、battle-axe分别匹配Conn与Shagga。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他不笼统许诺财富，而当场识别Conn、Shagga欲望与装备fit，试图把掠夺变成exchange。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “The halfman would pay us with our own coin,” said Conn.
blocks.explanation_zh: Conn指出Tyrion只是在用本就会被夺走的财物付款。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Conn拆穿现有财物不构成让步，迫使Tyrion提出他们无法单纯夺取的future value。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “Conn speaks truly,” Gunthor said. “Your silver is ours. Your horses are ours. Your hauberk and your battle-axe and the knife at your belt, those are ours too. You have nothing to give us but your lives. How would you like to die, Tyrion son of Tywin?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Gunthor同意，宣称所有物资已属于Stone Crows，只让Tyrion选择死法。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Stone Crows包围营地；Tyrion依次用hospitality、clan language、物资、幽默、羞耻与steel需求把处刑转为联盟谈判。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “In my own bed, with a belly full of wine and a maiden’s mouth around my cock, at the age of eighty,” he replied.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion以活到八十、wine与成人性享乐的粗俗愿望作答。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 粗俗死法回答拒绝接受Gunthor设定的即时恐惧，也用Shagga laughter打破一致敌意；这不是实际条件。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: The huge one, Shagga, laughed first and loudest. The others seemed less amused. “Conn, take their horses,” Gunthor commanded. “Kill the other and seize the halfinan. He can milk the goats and make the mothers laugh.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Shagga大笑，其他人不全买账；Gunthor命夺horses、杀Bronn、留Tyrion做低贱劳动和娱乐。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Stone Crows包围营地；Tyrion依次用hospitality、clan language、物资、幽默、羞耻与steel需求把处刑转为联盟谈判。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: Bronn sprang to his feet. “Who dies first?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Bronn跳起，问谁先死。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Stone Crows包围营地；Tyrion依次用hospitality、clan language、物资、幽默、羞耻与steel需求把处刑转为联盟谈判。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “No!” Tyrion said sharply. “Gunthor son of Gurn, hear me. My House is rich and powerful. If the Stone Crows will see us safely through these mountains, my lord father will shower you with gold.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion喝止战斗，提出若安全护送，Tywin会用gold奖赏Stone Crows。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tywin gold因不可验证、难送达而缺乏可信度；Tyrion下一步必须找到clans更急迫且可想象的价值。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “The gold of a lowland lord is as worthless as a halfman’s promises,” Gunthor said.
blocks.explanation_zh: Gunthor说lowland lord的gold与halfman promise一样无价值。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Stone Crows包围营地；Tyrion依次用hospitality、clan language、物资、幽默、羞耻与steel需求把处刑转为联盟谈判。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “Half a man I may be,” Tyrion said, “yet I have the courage to face my enemies. What do the Stone Crows do, but hide behind rocks and shiver with fear as the knights of the Vale ride by?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion转而用courage与羞耻激将，嘲Stone Crows躲石后怕Vale knights。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 当greed无效，Tyrion攻击group identity；这是高风险激将，目的在让他们证明勇气而继续听。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: Shagga gave a roar of anger and clashed club against axe. Jaggot poked at Tyrion’s face with the fire-hardened point of a long wooden spear. He did his best not to flinch. “Are these the best weapons you could steal?” he said. “Good enough for killing sheep, perhaps . . . if the sheep do not fight back. My father’s smiths shit better steel.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Shagga与Jaggot被激怒；Tyrion忍住spear威胁，又贬低他们偷来的weapons和steel。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 不flinch是表演成本：只有在spear近脸时仍贬低weapon，他的steel offer才显得不只是求饶话术。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: “Little boyman,” Shagga roared, “will you mock my axe after I chop off your manhood and feed it to the goats?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Shagga以针对Tyrion生殖器的暴力威胁回应其嘲讽。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Shagga以性化身体伤害威胁成人Tyrion；此处分析其恐吓升级，不美化或复述更多细节。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。

blocks.original_text: But Gunthor raised a hand. “No. I would hear his words. The mothers go hungry, and steel fills more mouths than gold. What would you give us for your lives, Tyrion son of Tywin? Swords? Lances? Mail?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Gunthor制止Shagga，因族中饥饿且steel比gold有用，询问Tyrion能给多少武器。
blocks.literary_note_zh: mothers hungry与steel fills mouths揭示实际约束：clan需要的不只是个人gold，而是能改变军事与生存结构的装备。
blocks.background_zh: **clan需求：** Gunthor明确说族中mothers挨饿，steel比gold更能获得食物；武器装备因此是核心谈判资源。

blocks.original_text: “All that, and more, Gunthor son of Gurn,” Tyrion Lannister replied, smiling. “I will give you the Vale of Arryn.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion微笑承诺swords、lances、mail以及更大报酬：整个Vale of Arryn。
blocks.literary_note_zh: give you the Vale把交易从escort fee升级为政治联盟提案；具体如何兑现仍完全未知，是chapter cliffhanger。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在high road风险、Bronn雇佣逻辑、Tyrion创伤记忆与Stone Crows谈判。
